commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7496488eb393641ba98d4116916e3652b7bdcbcf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Skill Workshop create/revise guard that rejects create proposals whose markdown references loaded workspace skill paths, plus agent/tool docs and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +82, Tests +146, Docs +10. Total +238 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main creates proposals from the submitted name and does not inspect proposal markdown for existing workspace skill paths; I did not run the MCP path because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review/contributor action is needed because the PR has a narrow correctness finding and lacks real behavior proof; automation should not repair while contributor proof is missing.

target.title: fix(skills): guard workshop creates from existing skill targets
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #94947.

`skill_workshop` could accept `action=create` proposals whose markdown clearly targeted existing workspace skill paths, so applying the proposal created an unintended sibling skill instead of routing the work through the existing skill update path.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a create-proposal guard that scans proposal content for workspace-relative `skills/...` path references and rejects the create when those paths resolve inside any discovered workspace skill directory. The guard uses the existing workspace skill loader so direct skills, grouped skills such as `skills/group/nested-skill/SKILL.md`, and non-canonical directory names are checked against the actual loaded skill roots.

The same guard also runs when revising a pending create proposal, so a harmless create draft cannot later be revised into a wrong-target existing-skill patch.

## User Impact

Agents now get an immediate, actionable error when `action=create` content points at existing workspace skills. The tool and Skill Workshop docs tell agents to use `action=update` with `skill_name` and split multi-skill changes into separate update proposals.

## Evidence

- Behavior addressed: `action=create` now rejects both initial create proposals and later create-proposal revisions when proposal content names an existing workspace skill path, including direct skills, grouped/nested skills, and non-canonical skill directory names.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree with the repo's Codex-worktree Vitest wrapper, plus GitHub's external-PR `Real behavior proof` check.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm install`; `pnpm docs:list`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/workshop/create-target-guard.test.ts src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/tools/skill-workshop-tool.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/tools/skill-workshop.md src/agents/skill-workshop-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/tools/skill-workshop-tool.ts src/skills/workshop/create-target-guard.ts src/skills/workshop/create-target-guard.test.ts src/skills/workshop/service.ts src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix: `create-target-guard.test.ts` builds real workspace skill fixtures under `skills/foo/SKILL.md`, `skills/group/nested-skill/SKILL.md`, and `skills/foo_bar/SKILL.md` and verifies existing-skill path refs are rejected while unrelated/new/url/prose refs are allowed. `service.test.ts` verifies `proposeCreateSkill` rejects existing-skill refs before creating a proposal, and `reviseSkillProposal` rejects revisions that would redirect a pending create proposal to an existing workspace skill without advancing the proposal version.
- Observed result after fix: the focused Vitest command passed in 3 shards covering 4 files / 134 tests; diff whitespace and oxfmt checks passed; GitHub `Real behavior proof` passed with `External PR includes problem context and evidence.`
- What was not tested: no full Testbox gate or live end-to-end agent session was run; this is a narrow Skill Workshop service/tool/prompt/docs patch covered by focused unit behavior tests and the external-PR proof check.
- Review proof: one Codex autoreview round found two P2 issues before this draft PR; both were fixed. Per local OpenClaw guardrails, no second autoreview loop was run for draft PR work.
target.author: monkeyleet
